title: One-Shot Bodies, Streaming, Uploads, Decompression, and Resource Limits · Automation and Data Client Sy… · Behavior Case: Streaming Uploads
summary: A climate archive streams large compressed files and uploads generated reports while bounding bytes, decompression, parsing, and temporary storage. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http py streaming uploads increment in a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers bounded streaming body pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Stream large uploads with known ownership, backpressure, progress, cancellation, and retryability. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Limit compressed and decoded response reads before allocation, parsing, logging, or persistence. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")
